prose:
Method-provenance summary
Property: Benzene frontier orbitals (HOMO, LUMO, and HOMO–LUMO gap)
Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (no density-fitting), evaluated with PySCF via chemkit
Geometry provenance: single-point calculation on the supplied benzene.xyz geometry (no further optimisation)
Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent)
Charge / multiplicity: 0 / 1 (closed-shell singlet)
Software: PySCF (version as packaged in chemkit engine)
SCF convergence: converged in 8 cycles; gradient/optimizer not applicable (single-point)
Warnings: no warnings reported by the engine
Live log: the SCF run was written live to a frontier_*.out file in the working directory (path shown at execution time via chemkit)
Integrity check: status ok, trustworthy = true (all automated checks passed)

Key results (eV)
HOMO energy = −7.0468329
LUMO energy = −0.41574728
HOMO–LUMO gap = 6.631085667557326
Vertical IP (Koopmans) = 7.0468329
Vertical EA (Koopmans) = 0.41574728

The frontier-orbital calculation was carried out with the chemkit “frontier-orbitals” skill invoking PySCF at the specified B3LYP/def2-TZVP level. The headline HOMO–LUMO gap is reported above (copying the tool’s value exactly, no rounding). No engine warnings were issued, and the run is marked trustworthy.
